chemistry.scope_summary: Proof-of-concept demonstration of [2 + 2] photocycloaddition reaction between maleimide and 1-hexyne under UV irradiation at ~280 nm using a fused silica microfluidic chip for continuous-flow photochemical synthesis

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: closely attached to the illumination surface

figures.key_information: Three-step fabrication process showing (i) fused silica substrate with serpentine channels, (ii) intermediate stage, and (iii) final device with inlets and outlet
figures.caption: Figure 1.  Schematic of the fabrication procedure for a 3D large-scale microchannel chip with two inlets and one outlet in fused silica. It consists of three main steps: ( i ) ultrafast laser direct writing of 3D modiﬁed micropatterns in glass; ( ii ) selective chemical etching of hollow glass microstructures including 3D microchannels, micromixing units, and open extra-access ports; and ( iii ) d

figures.flow_path: 2 inlets converging to serpentine channels leading to 1 outlet
figures.light_source_position: external UV LED array positioned below the chip
figures.key_information: Photochemical [2+2] cycloaddition reaction between maleimide and 1-hexyne using 280 nm UV irradiation in MeCN solvent, with NMR analysis showing product formation only with UV irradiation
figures.caption: Figure 5.  ( a ) Demonstration of high-efficiency mixing in a fused silica microfluidic chip with a size  of 100 mm × 100 mm × 3 mm using different dye solutions. Left: yellow and blue dye solutions were  pumped into two inlets with a flow rate of 20 mL/min, respectively; Right: red and blue dye solu- tions were pumped into two inlets with the same flow rate, respectively. ( b ) a [2 + 2] photocyc

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous-flow UV photochemical synthesis using fused silica microfluidic chips enables high-efficiency mixing and precise control over reaction conditions
translation_logic.what_changed: Implementation of microfluidic flow chemistry instead of batch conditions
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: [2+2] photocycloaddition chemistry between maleimide and 1-hexyne with UV light at 280 nm
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Microfluidic containment of UV photochemical reaction

figure_descriptions: Photochemical reaction setup with UV LED irradiation

conflicts: Figure caption mentions flow rate of 20 mL/min for dye demonstration but text indicates 0.2 mL/min total flow rate for reaction
notes: This is a proof-of-concept study focusing on microfluidic device fabrication rather than systematic optimization. No batch baseline comparison provided. Limited quantitative reaction data available.
